# archflow llms-full.txt

Generated by `npm run llms:gen` from README.md, the public JSON Schema, cookbook docs, and cookbook YAML examples.

---

# archflow

> Open-source interactive architecture diagrams with simulated request flows.

**Status:** Foundation slice (Plan 1). The full app, docs, and live demo land in Plan 5.

## Run locally

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

Open the printed URL, click ▶ Play, watch the bolinhas flow.

## Test

```bash
npm test
```

## License

MIT — see `LICENSE`.

---

# Compact JSON Schema reference

Schema URL: https://example.com/archflow/schema/archflow.schema.json

Required fields by node `type`:

- `client`: type, id, rps
- `webhook`: type, id, rps, pattern
- `load_balancer`: type, id, strategy
- `gateway`: type, id, rate_limit_rps
- `service`: type, id
- `worker`: type, id, concurrency, latency_ms, error_rate
- `queue`: type, id, max_depth
- `cache`: type, id, hit_rate, latency_ms
- `database`: type, id, pool_size, query_latency_ms, timeout_ms
- `cluster`: type, id

---

# Cookbook docs and YAML examples

# 01 E-commerce

A richer e-commerce topology showing browse, cart, and checkout flows through an edge gateway, app load balancer, catalog/cart/payment services, Redis-style product cache, Postgres, and asynchronous fulfillment workers.

```text
[Shoppers] -> [Gateway] -> [LB] -> [Catalog] -> [Product Cache] -> [DB]
                              |-> [Cart] -----------------------> [DB]
                              |-> [Payment] -> [Fulfillment Q] -> [Worker] -> [DB]
```

| Name | Type | Key params |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Web + Mobile Shoppers | client | 80 rps, constant, payload 4 |
| Edge API Gateway | gateway | 250 rps rate limit, 3 ms auth |
| App Load Balancer | load_balancer | least_conn |
| Catalog API | service | 25 ms latency, 120 rps capacity |
| Cart API | service | 35 ms latency, 90 rps capacity |
| Payment API | service | 80 ms latency, 45 rps capacity |
| Product Cache | cache | 82% hit rate, 2 ms latency |
| Commerce Postgres | database | pool 35, 12 ms query latency |
| Fulfillment Queue | queue | depth 1500, dlq overflow |
| Fulfillment Worker Pool | worker | concurrency 6, hourly reconcile trigger |

Scenarios split shopper traffic into `browse`, `cart`, and `checkout` tags so the simulator routes most traffic to catalog/cache, some to cart/database, and checkout traffic through payment into asynchronous fulfillment.

[Open in app](?example=01-ecommerce)

## YAML

```yaml
$schema: "https://example.com/archflow/schema/archflow.schema.json"
version: 1
nodes:
  - type: client
    id: shoppers
    label: Web + Mobile Shoppers
    rps: 80
    pattern: constant
    payload_size: 4
    position: { x: 60, y: 150 }
  - type: gateway
    id: edge-gateway
    label: Edge API Gateway
    rate_limit_rps: 250
    auth_check_ms: 3
    position: { x: 260, y: 150 }
  - type: load_balancer
    id: app-lb
    label: App Load Balancer
    strategy: least_conn
    position: { x: 460, y: 150 }
  - type: service
    id: catalog-api
    label: Catalog API
    latency_ms: 25
    capacity_rps: 120
    error_rate: 0.005
    position: { x: 680, y: 60 }
  - type: service
    id: cart-api
    label: Cart API
    latency_ms: 35
    capacity_rps: 90
    error_rate: 0.01
    position: { x: 680, y: 180 }
  - type: service
    id: payment-api
    label: Payment API
    latency_ms: 80
    capacity_rps: 45
    error_rate: 0.02
    position: { x: 680, y: 320 }
  - type: cache
    id: product-cache
    label: Product Cache
    hit_rate: 0.82
    latency_ms: 2
    position: { x: 910, y: 60 }
  - type: database
    id: commerce-db
    label: Commerce Postgres
    pool_size: 35
    query_latency_ms: 12
    timeout_ms: 250
    position: { x: 1130, y: 180 }
  - type: queue
    id: fulfillment-queue
    label: Fulfillment Queue
    max_depth: 1500
    on_overflow: dlq
    position: { x: 910, y: 320 }
  - type: worker
    id: fulfillment-worker
    label: Fulfillment Worker Pool
    concurrency: 6
    latency_ms: 140
    error_rate: 0.01
    triggers:
      - id: hourly-reconcile
        cron: "0 * * * *"
        payload_size: 2
    position: { x: 1130, y: 320 }
edges:
  - id: shoppers-to-gateway
    source: shoppers
    target: edge-gateway
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 8
  - id: gateway-to-lb
    source: edge-gateway
    target: app-lb
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 2
  - id: lb-to-catalog
    source: app-lb
    target: catalog-api
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 3
    weight: 3
    tags: [scenario:browse]
  - id: lb-to-cart
    source: app-lb
    target: cart-api
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 4
    weight: 2
    tags: [scenario:cart]
  - id: lb-to-payment
    source: app-lb
    target: payment-api
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 6
    weight: 1
    tags: [scenario:checkout]
  - id: catalog-to-cache
    source: catalog-api
    target: product-cache
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 1
    tags: [tag:hit, scenario:browse]
  - id: catalog-to-db
    source: catalog-api
    target: commerce-db
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 10
    tags: [tag:miss]
  - id: cart-to-db
    source: cart-api
    target: commerce-db
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 10
  - id: payment-to-gateway
    source: payment-api
    target: fulfillment-queue
    kind: async
    latency_ms: 4
    tags: [scenario:checkout]
  - id: queue-to-worker
    source: fulfillment-queue
    target: fulfillment-worker
    kind: async
    latency_ms: 2
  - id: worker-to-db
    source: fulfillment-worker
    target: commerce-db
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 12
scenarios:
  - id: browse
    origin: shoppers
    color: "#2dd4bf"
    weight: 4
  - id: cart
    origin: shoppers
    color: "#60a5fa"
    weight: 2
  - id: checkout
    origin: shoppers
    color: "#f97316"
    weight: 1
```

---

# 02 Microservices Async

A producer service accepts bursty events, buffers them in a queue, and drains work through a worker pool with concurrency 4 into a database; the high arrival rate intentionally demonstrates backpressure.

```text
[Event Clients] -> [Producer Service] -> [Jobs Queue] -> [Worker Pool x4] -> [Events DB]
```

| Name | Type | Key params |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Event Clients | client | 420 rps, burst pattern |
| Producer Service | service | 18 ms latency, 220 rps capacity |
| Jobs Queue | queue | depth 800, dlq overflow |
| Worker Pool x4 | worker | concurrency 4, 180 ms latency |
| Events DB | database | pool 18, 18 ms query latency |

The `backpressure` scenario starts at the event clients. Because arrivals exceed worker drain capacity, queue depth grows and overflow behavior becomes visible.

[Open in app](?example=02-microservices-async)

## YAML

```yaml
$schema: "https://example.com/archflow/schema/archflow.schema.json"
version: 1
nodes:
  - type: client
    id: event-clients
    label: Event Clients
    rps: 420
    pattern: burst
    payload_size: 1
    position: { x: 60, y: 180 }
  - type: service
    id: producer-api
    label: Producer Service
    latency_ms: 18
    capacity_rps: 220
    error_rate: 0.005
    position: { x: 280, y: 180 }
  - type: queue
    id: jobs-queue
    label: Jobs Queue
    max_depth: 800
    on_overflow: dlq
    position: { x: 520, y: 180 }
  - type: worker
    id: worker-pool
    label: Worker Pool x4
    concurrency: 4
    latency_ms: 180
    error_rate: 0.01
    position: { x: 760, y: 180 }
  - type: database
    id: events-db
    label: Events DB
    pool_size: 18
    query_latency_ms: 18
    timeout_ms: 300
    position: { x: 1000, y: 180 }
edges:
  - id: clients-to-producer
    source: event-clients
    target: producer-api
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 6
  - id: producer-to-queue
    source: producer-api
    target: jobs-queue
    kind: async
    latency_ms: 3
  - id: queue-to-workers
    source: jobs-queue
    target: worker-pool
    kind: async
    latency_ms: 2
  - id: workers-to-db
    source: worker-pool
    target: events-db
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 12
scenarios:
  - id: backpressure
    origin: event-clients
    color: "#ef4444"
    weight: 1
```

---

# 03 API Gateway

A public API gateway performs rate limiting and JWT-like auth checks before forwarding to a load balancer and three downstream services backed by a shared cache and database.

```text
[Public Users] -> [Rate-limit + JWT Gateway] -> [LB] -> [Users Service]  -> [Shared Cache] -> [DB]
                                                   |-> [Orders Service] -> [Shared Cache] -> [DB]
                                                   |-> [Search Service] -> [Shared Cache] -> [DB]
```

| Name | Type | Key params |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Public Users | client | 180 rps, constant |
| Rate-limit + JWT Gateway | gateway | 150 rps rate limit, 8 ms auth |
| Service Load Balancer | load_balancer | round_robin |
| Users Service | service | 30 ms latency, 70 rps capacity |
| Orders Service | service | 45 ms latency, 65 rps capacity |
| Search Service | service | 55 ms latency, 90 rps capacity |
| Shared Redis Cache | cache | 76% hit rate, 2 ms latency |
| Shared Database | database | pool 32, 14 ms query latency |

Scenarios model `profile`, `orders`, and `search` requests with weighted routing through the gateway and load balancer. Cache hit/miss tags show how misses fall through to the database.

[Open in app](?example=03-api-gateway)

## YAML

```yaml
$schema: "https://example.com/archflow/schema/archflow.schema.json"
version: 1
nodes:
  - type: client
    id: public-users
    label: Public Users
    rps: 180
    pattern: constant
    payload_size: 2
    position: { x: 60, y: 190 }
  - type: gateway
    id: api-gateway
    label: Rate-limit + JWT Gateway
    rate_limit_rps: 150
    auth_check_ms: 8
    position: { x: 300, y: 190 }
  - type: load_balancer
    id: service-lb
    label: Service Load Balancer
    strategy: round_robin
    position: { x: 540, y: 190 }
  - type: service
    id: users-service
    label: Users Service
    latency_ms: 30
    capacity_rps: 70
    error_rate: 0.005
    position: { x: 780, y: 70 }
  - type: service
    id: orders-service
    label: Orders Service
    latency_ms: 45
    capacity_rps: 65
    error_rate: 0.01
    position: { x: 780, y: 190 }
  - type: service
    id: search-service
    label: Search Service
    latency_ms: 55
    capacity_rps: 90
    error_rate: 0.008
    position: { x: 780, y: 310 }
  - type: cache
    id: shared-cache
    label: Shared Redis Cache
    hit_rate: 0.76
    latency_ms: 2
    position: { x: 1040, y: 120 }
  - type: database
    id: api-db
    label: Shared Database
    pool_size: 32
    query_latency_ms: 14
    timeout_ms: 220
    position: { x: 1040, y: 280 }
edges:
  - id: users-to-gateway
    source: public-users
    target: api-gateway
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 8
  - id: gateway-to-lb
    source: api-gateway
    target: service-lb
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 2
  - id: lb-to-users
    source: service-lb
    target: users-service
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 3
    weight: 1
    tags: [scenario:profile]
  - id: lb-to-orders
    source: service-lb
    target: orders-service
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 4
    weight: 2
    tags: [scenario:orders]
  - id: lb-to-search
    source: service-lb
    target: search-service
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 5
    weight: 3
    tags: [scenario:search]
  - id: users-to-cache
    source: users-service
    target: shared-cache
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 1
    tags: [tag:hit]
  - id: orders-to-cache
    source: orders-service
    target: shared-cache
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 1
    tags: [tag:hit]
  - id: search-to-cache
    source: search-service
    target: shared-cache
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 1
    tags: [tag:hit]
  - id: cache-to-db
    source: shared-cache
    target: api-db
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 10
    tags: [tag:miss]
scenarios:
  - id: profile
    origin: public-users
    color: "#a78bfa"
    weight: 1
  - id: orders
    origin: public-users
    color: "#38bdf8"
    weight: 2
  - id: search
    origin: public-users
    color: "#22c55e"
    weight: 3
```

---

# 04 K8s Autoscaling

A bursting client drives traffic through an ingress and frontend to an API service inside a Kubernetes cluster with HPA enabled from 1 to 10 replicas at a 60% CPU target.

```text
                 +-- Production Kubernetes ----------------+
[Burst Client] -> [Ingress] -> [Frontend] -> [API + HPA] -> [State DB]
                 +------------------------------------------+
```

| Name | Type | Key params |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Production Kubernetes | cluster | members: frontend, api-service |
| Burst Traffic Client | client | 360 rps, burst pattern |
| Ingress Gateway | gateway | 500 rps rate limit, 2 ms auth |
| Frontend Service | service | 20 ms latency, 180 rps capacity |
| API Service with HPA | service | min 1, max 10, target_cpu_pct 60 |
| State Database | database | pool 25, 16 ms query latency |

The `burst-scaleout` scenario demonstrates CPU utilization rising under burst traffic. The HPA should add replicas during sustained load and stabilize after the burst.

[Open in app](?example=04-k8s-autoscaling)

## YAML

```yaml
$schema: "https://example.com/archflow/schema/archflow.schema.json"
version: 1
nodes:
  - type: cluster
    id: prod-cluster
    label: Production Kubernetes
    members: [frontend, api-service]
    position: { x: 390, y: 70 }
  - type: client
    id: burst-client
    label: Burst Traffic Client
    rps: 360
    pattern: burst
    payload_size: 2
    position: { x: 80, y: 210 }
  - type: gateway
    id: ingress
    label: Ingress Gateway
    rate_limit_rps: 500
    auth_check_ms: 2
    position: { x: 310, y: 210 }
  - type: service
    id: frontend
    label: Frontend Service
    latency_ms: 20
    capacity_rps: 180
    error_rate: 0.002
    position: { x: 540, y: 170 }
  - type: service
    id: api-service
    label: API Service with HPA
    latency_ms: 50
    capacity_rps: 80
    error_rate: 0.006
    resources:
      cpu_per_request_ms: 55
      mem_per_request_mb: 3
      cpu_limit_ms_per_sec: 1000
      mem_limit_mb: 512
    hpa:
      min_replicas: 1
      max_replicas: 10
      target_cpu_pct: 60
      stabilization_ticks: 5
    position: { x: 780, y: 210 }
  - type: database
    id: state-db
    label: State Database
    pool_size: 25
    query_latency_ms: 16
    timeout_ms: 250
    position: { x: 1020, y: 210 }
edges:
  - id: client-to-ingress
    source: burst-client
    target: ingress
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 7
  - id: ingress-to-frontend
    source: ingress
    target: frontend
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 3
  - id: frontend-to-api
    source: frontend
    target: api-service
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 4
  - id: api-to-db
    source: api-service
    target: state-db
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 12
scenarios:
  - id: burst-scaleout
    origin: burst-client
    color: "#f59e0b"
    weight: 1
```

---

# 05 Event Driven

An event-driven webhook ingestion pipeline combines poisson webhook arrivals with a scheduled batch trigger, queues events, fans out to projection and cache invalidation workers, and persists to an event store.

```text
[Partner Webhook] -> [Webhook Ingestor] -> [Event Queue] -> [Projection Worker] -> [Event Store DB]
                                             |-----------> [Cache Invalidator] -> [Read Cache] -> [Event Store DB]
```

| Name | Type | Key params |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Partner Webhook | webhook | 65 rps, poisson pattern |
| Webhook Ingestor | service | 22 ms latency, nightly cron trigger |
| Event Queue | queue | depth 1200, dlq overflow |
| Projection Worker Fan-out | worker | concurrency 8, 90 ms latency |
| Cache Invalidation Worker | worker | concurrency 3, 45 ms latency |
| Read Model Cache | cache | 88% hit rate, 2 ms latency |
| Event Store DB | database | pool 28, 20 ms query latency |

Scenarios split queued events into `projection` and `invalidation` flows. The service trigger `nightly-batch` adds cron-driven load alongside webhook poisson arrivals.

[Open in app](?example=05-event-driven)

## YAML

```yaml
$schema: "https://example.com/archflow/schema/archflow.schema.json"
version: 1
nodes:
  - type: webhook
    id: partner-webhook
    label: Partner Webhook
    rps: 65
    pattern: poisson
    position: { x: 60, y: 160 }
  - type: service
    id: webhook-ingestor
    label: Webhook Ingestor
    latency_ms: 22
    capacity_rps: 140
    error_rate: 0.004
    triggers:
      - id: nightly-batch
        cron: "30 2 * * *"
        payload_size: 3
    position: { x: 280, y: 160 }
  - type: queue
    id: event-bus
    label: Event Queue
    max_depth: 1200
    on_overflow: dlq
    position: { x: 500, y: 160 }
  - type: worker
    id: projection-worker
    label: Projection Worker Fan-out
    concurrency: 8
    latency_ms: 90
    error_rate: 0.008
    position: { x: 740, y: 100 }
  - type: worker
    id: cache-invalidator
    label: Cache Invalidation Worker
    concurrency: 3
    latency_ms: 45
    error_rate: 0.004
    position: { x: 740, y: 250 }
  - type: cache
    id: read-cache
    label: Read Model Cache
    hit_rate: 0.88
    latency_ms: 2
    position: { x: 980, y: 250 }
  - type: database
    id: event-store
    label: Event Store DB
    pool_size: 28
    query_latency_ms: 20
    timeout_ms: 300
    position: { x: 980, y: 100 }
edges:
  - id: webhook-to-ingestor
    source: partner-webhook
    target: webhook-ingestor
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 5
  - id: ingestor-to-queue
    source: webhook-ingestor
    target: event-bus
    kind: async
    latency_ms: 3
  - id: queue-to-projections
    source: event-bus
    target: projection-worker
    kind: async
    latency_ms: 2
    weight: 3
    tags: [scenario:projection]
  - id: queue-to-cache-invalidator
    source: event-bus
    target: cache-invalidator
    kind: async
    latency_ms: 2
    weight: 1
    tags: [scenario:invalidation]
  - id: projections-to-db
    source: projection-worker
    target: event-store
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 14
  - id: invalidator-to-cache
    source: cache-invalidator
    target: read-cache
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 2
  - id: cache-to-event-store
    source: read-cache
    target: event-store
    kind: sync
    latency_ms: 12
    tags: [tag:miss]
scenarios:
  - id: projection
    origin: partner-webhook
    color: "#14b8a6"
    weight: 3
  - id: invalidation
    origin: partner-webhook
    color: "#e879f9"
    weight: 1
```
